notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix pipeline. RCA at deliver/rca.md is the authoritative specification for this work — it was user-reviewed and approved in Phase 2 of /nw-bugfix before roadmap creation.

Scope: operator CLI computes two inconsistent paths on the $HOME fallback branch. `cluster init` writes to $HOME/.overdrive/.overdrive/config (doubled segment, Bug A at commands/cluster.rs:174). The read side at main.rs:148 computes $HOME/.overdrive/config correctly, BUT its doc comment at main.rs:140-141 lies — documenting the doubled form as expected behaviour (Bug B is a documentation + drift-surface bug; the code already computes the correct path). Both stem from raw PathBuf carrying implicit `is-this-already-suffixed?` semantics that the type system does not enforce, and from two sibling functions computing the canonical path independently.

Three fixes in scope (all shipped together in this step per user confirmation — Fix 3 is in-scope, not deferred). Fix 1: commands/cluster.rs:174 — change `Ok(PathBuf::from(home).join(".overdrive"))` so resolve_config_dir returns a base dir (write_trust_triple already owns the .overdrive/config suffix). Fix 2: main.rs:140-151 — rewrite default_config_path against the shared helper from Fix 3 and replace the lying doc comment. Fix 3: extract `pub fn default_operator_config_path()` in overdrive_cli::commands::cluster (or new overdrive_cli::config module at crafter's discretion) that encodes the canonical $OVERDRIVE_CONFIG_DIR → $HOME → . fallback and appends .overdrive/config exactly once; both main.rs and cluster.rs's HOME fallback delegate to it. Fix 3 collapses the drift surface structurally so this class of bug cannot recur.

Env-var serialisation choice (user-confirmed): `serial_test` crate with #[serial(env)] annotation — lowest coupling, dev-dep only, no test-binary split. Rust edition 2024 + rust-version 1.85 + workspace-wide `unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn = deny` requires explicit unsafe{} around env::set_var / env::remove_var.

Single-step TDD per testing.md §RED scaffolds ("bundle with the GREEN commit if the crafter judges a single commit cleaner") and user memory `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`. Test + fix land in one commit so the reviewer sees the coupling; no --no-verify exception needed. The RED→GREEN transition is verifiable within the step: the crafter runs the test BEFORE applying the fix (must fail), then AFTER (must pass).

No new ADR required — ADR-0010, ADR-0014, and ADR-0019 already specify `~/.overdrive/config` as the canonical operator config path. This is a conformance fix against existing spec, not a design change.

Mutation testing scope: diff-scoped via `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-cli` — kill-rate gate ≥80% per project policy. Diff is ~15 LOC across 2 source files + test scaffolding; mutant count will be small.

reviews.scope: RCA + fix direction + regression test design + env-var serialisation choice (serial_test) + Fix 3 in-scope decision
reviews.notes: User confirmed: (1) root cause including read/write-path asymmetry matches mental model, (2) Fix 3 is in-scope (not deferred), (3) serial_test is the env-mutation serialisation mechanism.

phases.name: Conformance fix for $HOME-fallback config path (RED regression test + GREEN fix in one commit)
phases.description: Write a regression test on the $HOME fallback branch that FAILS on current code (proves Bug A). Apply Fix 1 + Fix 2 + Fix 3 so the test passes and the read/write sites call one shared helper. All pre-existing tests continue to pass.

steps.name: Regression test + Fix 1+2+3 (one TDD cycle, one commit)
steps.criteria: Test lands in crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/cluster_init_serve.rs: `resolve_config_dir_home_fallback_writes_at_canonical_path` annotated `#[tokio::test]` + `#[serial_test::serial(env)]`. Body: create TempDir; save $HOME + $OVERDRIVE_CONFIG_DIR; `unsafe { std::env::set_var("HOME", tmp.path()); std::env::remove_var("OVERDRIVE_CONFIG_DIR"); }`; invoke `overdrive_cli::commands::cluster::init(InitArgs { config_dir: None, force: false })`; assert (a) `tmp.path().join(".overdrive").join("config")` exists, (b) `tmp.path().join(".overdrive").join(".overdrive").join("config")` does NOT exist (explicit regression guard — this is the doubled-segment case), (c) returned `InitOutput::config_path` equals the canonical path; restore env in unsafe{}. Optionally add a second smaller test `default_config_path_matches_init_write_location_on_home_fallback` that asserts the shared helper from Fix 3 returns a path equal to what init writes — catches future drift if the two sites ever diverge again. serial_test added to workspace deps: root Cargo.toml `[workspace.dependencies]` under `# --- Testing ---` section add `serial_test = "3"` (pin nearest stable major), crates/overdrive-cli/Cargo.toml `[dev-dependencies]` add `serial_test.workspace = true`. Fix 1 (cluster.rs:174): return `Ok(PathBuf::from(home))` instead of the `.join(".overdrive")` form; update doc comment at line 157 so it documents the return as a BASE directory (write_trust_triple owns the .overdrive suffix). Fix 3 (new pub fn): extract `pub fn default_operator_config_path() -> PathBuf` into commands::cluster (or new `overdrive_cli::config` module — crafter's call based on what reads cleanly) that encodes the $OVERDRIVE_CONFIG_DIR → $HOME → `.` fallback and appends `.overdrive/config` exactly once. Fix 2 (main.rs:140-151): replace default_config_path body with a thin delegate to the Fix 3 helper; replace the lying doc comment with correct spec per ADR-0019 (`~/.overdrive/config`, single segment). Verification: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests` — new regression tests pass; all pre-existing tests still pass. `cargo clippy -p overdrive-cli --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` clean on touched files. Commit message: `fix(cli): write operator config to ~/.overdrive/config (single segment)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer. No --no-verify (normal pre-commit hooks pass).
steps.scenario_name: Operator CLI writes trust triple to canonical $HOME/.overdrive/config on HOME fallback; read and write sites compute the same path via a shared helper
steps.tier: Tier 1 (acceptance / integration — requires integration-tests feature per testing.md)
steps.notes: Context: deliver/rca.md — full RCA including code-level before/after for all three fixes. Bug sites verified on HEAD: cluster.rs:174 returns the suffixed form; main.rs:140-141 doc comment lies about `$HOME/.overdrive/.overdrive/config` as expected (code at 148 actually computes the single-segment form correctly; the lie is in the documentation); tls_bootstrap.rs:296/301 confirms write_trust_triple joins `.overdrive/config` onto config_dir. TDD ordering within this single step: (1) PREPARE — read the RCA; verify bug sites on current HEAD; confirm test file + Cargo.toml patterns. (2) RED_ACCEPTANCE — write the regression test(s); add serial_test dep; run `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests -E 'test(resolve_config_dir_home_fallback_writes_at_canonical_path)'` — MUST fail; the failure message must be the canonical vs doubled path assertion, NOT an import/type error. (3) RED_UNIT — SKIPPED (NOT_APPLICABLE: acceptance-level regression test covers the structural invariant; a unit test on the helper would be a tautology). (4) GREEN — apply Fix 3 (new shared helper), then Fix 1 (cluster.rs delegate), then Fix 2 (main.rs delegate + comment). Re-run the regression test — MUST pass. Run full cluster-init-serve suite — MUST still pass. (5) COMMIT — `git add`; commit with the conventional message + Step-ID trailer. Post-GREEN wiring smoke check per testing.md: `git diff --name-only` shows cluster.rs AND main.rs modified (Fix 3's new helper must actually be called from both sites). The operator's existing broken-path file at `~/.overdrive/.overdrive/config` is NOT auto-migrated; mention in the commit body that operators can `mv` the file or re-run `cluster init` (Phase 1 CA is ephemeral per ADR-0010 §R4). Record all DES phases via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase` per the template — do NOT edit execution-log.json directly.

walking_skeleton.description: After 01-01 commits: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests` all green, including the new regression test(s). On the user's laptop, re-running `overdrive cluster init` writes to $HOME/.overdrive/config (single segment) rather than the doubled path.

per_step: Regression test fails BEFORE the fix is applied (RED verified)
Regression test passes AFTER the fix is applied (GREEN verified)
All pre-existing tests continue to pass (no regressions)
No unjustified clippy warnings on touched files
post_deliver: Diff-scoped mutation testing: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-cli` with ≥80% kill rate (rigor.mutation_enabled = true, .claude/rules/testing.md per-feature policy)

tdd_rhythm_per_step: Canonical 5-phase cycle within one step: PREPARE → RED_ACCEPTANCE (write failing test, verify RED) → RED_UNIT (SKIPPED, NOT_APPLICABLE) → GREEN (apply Fix 1+2+3, verify GREEN) → COMMIT (single conventional commit, Step-ID trailer). No --no-verify; normal pre-commit hooks apply.

validation.notes: Phase 2 of /nw-bugfix is the user review gate. User approved: root cause match, Fix 3 in scope, serial_test serialisation mechanism. Roadmap reflects those decisions.
